2604.04852.title: Strengthening Human-Centric Chain-of-Thought Reasoning Integrity in LLMs via a Structured Prompt Framework
2604.04852.paper_summary: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting has been used to enhance the reasoning capability of LLMs. However, its reliability in security-sensitive analytical tasks remains insufficiently examined, particularly under structured human evaluation. Alternative approaches, such as model scaling and fine-tuning can be used to help improve performance. These methods are also often costly, computationally intensive, or difficult to audit. In contrast, prompt engineering provides a lightweight, transparent, and controllable mechanism for guiding LLM reasoning. This study proposes a structured prompt engineering framework designed to strengthen CoT reasoning integrity while improving security threat and attack detection reliability in local LLM deployments. The framework includes 16 factors grouped into four core dimensions: (1) Context and Scope Control, (2) Evidence Grounding and Traceability, (3) Reasoning Structure and Cognitive Control, and (4) Security-Specific Analytical Constraints. Rather than optimizing the wording of the prompt heuristically, the framework introduces explicit reasoning controls to mitigate hallucination and prevent reasoning drift, as well as strengthening interpretability in security-sensitive contexts. Using DDoS attack detection in SDN traffic as a case study, multiple model families were evaluated under structured and unstructured prompting conditions. Pareto frontier analysis and ablation experiments demonstrate consistent reasoning improvements (up to 40% in smaller models) and stable accuracy gains across scales. Human evaluation with strong inter-rater agreement (Cohen's k > 0.80) confirms robustness. The results establish structured prompting as an effective and practical approach for reliable and explainable AI-driven cybersecurity analysis.
2604.04852.paper_summary_zh: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) 提示已被用來增強大型語言模型（LLMs）的推理能力。<lb/>然而，在安全敏感的分析任務中，其可靠性仍然不足，特別是在結構化的人類評估下。<lb/>替代方法，如模型擴展和微調，可以用來幫助提高性能。<lb/>這些方法通常也很昂貴、計算密集或難以審計。<lb/>相比之下，提示工程提供了一種輕量級、透明且可控的機制來引導 LLM 的推理。<lb/>本研究提出了一個結構化的提示工程框架，旨在加強 CoT 推理的完整性，同時提高本地 LLM 部署中的安全威脅和攻擊檢測的可靠性。<lb/>該框架包括 16 個因素，分為四個核心維度：<lb/>(1) 上下文和範圍控制，<lb/>(2) 證據基礎和可追溯性，<lb/>(3) 推理結構和認知控制，以及 <lb/>(4) 安全特定的分析約束。<lb/>該框架不是通過啟發式地優化提示的措辭，而是引入明確的推理控制來減輕幻覺，防止推理漂移，並加強在安全敏感環境中的可解釋性。<lb/>以 SDN 流量中的 DDoS 攻擊檢測為案例研究，評估了多個模型家族在結構化和非結構化提示條件下的表現。<lb/>帕累托邊界分析和消融實驗顯示出一致的推理改進（在較小模型中可達 40%）和跨規模的穩定準確性增益。<lb/>人類評估顯示出強烈的評分者間一致性（Cohen's k > 0.80），確認了其穩健性。<lb/>結果確立了結構化提示作為一種有效且實用的方法，用於可靠且可解釋的 AI 驅動的網絡安全分析。
2604.04852.author: Jiling Zhou et.al.
2604.04852.authors: Jiling Zhou, Aisvarya Adeseye, Seppo Virtanen, Antti Hakkala, Jouni Isoaho
